formats.audience_experience: A reader follows a sequence of connected posts and may respond at any point.
limitations: weak transitions make the sequence feel fragmented
too many points dilute the core claim
production_demands: each post must stand alone while serving the whole
formats.structure_notes: Open with a complete claim, develop one idea per post, then land the argument. Number only when navigation benefits.
formats.skeleton:
Post 1: [complete hook]
Posts 2-N: [one claim, example, or step each]
Final post: [landing or invitation]
reuse_pathways: A single line may later become a separate X post; a visual sequence may later be adapted deliberately.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

formats.audience_experience: A reader encounters one self-contained thought in a fast text feed.
expressive_strengths: lands one idea with maximum concentration
makes a claim easy to repeat
limitations: compression can turn nuance into empty provocation
production_demands: one strong sentence or compact paragraph
formats.structure_notes: Lead with the thought itself. Keep one idea. Add context only when it strengthens rather than explains away the line.
reuse_pathways: A proven line may later seed a longer treatment, but no derivative is required.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

formats.audience_experience: A reader makes a structured choice, then sees or discusses the group response.
limitations: complex views do not fit fixed choices
formats.structure_notes: Ask one non-obvious question. Keep options balanced and mutually intelligible. Treat results as audience evidence, not universal truth.
reuse_pathways: Results may justify a separate analysis after voting closes.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

formats.audience_experience: A viewer swipes through a controlled visual sequence at their own pace.
formats.structure_notes: The cover earns the swipe. Each slide advances one beat. Use visual hierarchy and keep every page comprehensible on mobile.
formats.skeleton:
Slide 1: [cover]
Slides 2-N: [one beat each]
Final slide: [landing or next action]
capture_tasks: Capture source-specific photography when visual proof materially strengthens the idea.
reuse_pathways: An individual slide may stand alone later only if it remains truthful outside the sequence.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

formats.audience_experience: A viewer experiences a short vertical audiovisual piece in a continuous discovery feed.
expressive_strengths: uses voice and timing for emotional delivery
carries a spoken argument with visual support — the pipeline generates visuals from the script, so production effort is not a barrier to choosing this format
preferred format when the idea has a strong spoken arc, a personal angle, or benefits from pacing and voice
limitations: weak visuals become a text post read aloud
forced movement distracts from the idea
production_demands: clear audio when speech is used
formats.structure_notes: Open with immediate substance, develop one coherent idea, and let motion or voice do work that static text cannot.
formats.skeleton:
HOOK: [visual/spoken entry]
DEVELOPMENT: [one coherent progression]
LANDING: [memorable close without padding]
capture_tasks: Record talking-head delivery or relevant original footage only when the chosen treatment calls for human presence.
reuse_pathways: A strong spoken line may later be adapted separately; the Reel does not require a matching X post.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

formats.audience_experience: A viewer receives one visual statement, with optional depth in the caption.
expressive_strengths: makes a quote or image immediately legible
production_demands: one strong image or designed composition
formats.length: One image; caption length follows the idea
formats.structure_notes: The image must communicate before the caption. Use the caption to deepen, not rescue, the visual.
formats.skeleton:
Image: [single visual statement]
Caption: [optional context and landing]
capture_tasks: Capture a real photograph when authenticity or evidence is central.
reuse_pathways: The visual may be reused only where its meaning and dimensions remain native.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

formats.audience_experience: A viewer taps through temporary vertical frames and can respond through native stickers.
limitations: small text is hard to read
production_demands: native interaction where it serves the idea
formats.structure_notes: Each frame must work quickly while advancing the sequence. Use interaction only when the response matters.
formats.skeleton:
Frame 1: [entry]
Frames 2-N: [progression or interaction]
Final frame: [landing/link/reply invitation]
capture_tasks: Capture real-time footage when immediacy is part of the treatment.
reuse_pathways: Audience responses may become research input; no feed derivative is automatic.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

formats.audience_experience: A reader spends sustained attention on a structured long-form argument or report.
limitations: not all social surfaces support the same reading experience
formats.structure_notes: Use a clear opening question or claim, develop sourced sections, and land with a conclusion earned by the body.
formats.skeleton:
Opening: [specific question/claim]
Sections: [evidence and development]
Close: [synthesis or invitation]
reuse_pathways: Specific excerpts may later inspire independent social pieces; adaptation is a separate editorial decision.

performance_evidence.notes: General platform prior only; StackPenni tenant performance has not yet validated this entry.

formats.provenance: Migrated from Format Guide v1.0 under user approval; affordances derived from native platform mechanics.

summary: A descriptive catalogue of StackPenni formats. Selection is contextual: the user may constrain a platform or exact format; otherwise the LLM chooses one primary destination by matching an idea’s expressive needs, evidence, audience experience, and production feasibility to these affordances. No message-type routing table applies.
